articles.title: EC2’s formally verified “isolation engine” provides mathematical assurance of virtual-machine isolation
articles.summary: Splitting the “separation kernel” off from the rest of the Nitro security system and using only a subset of the Rust programming language to code it enabled its formal verification.

articles.title: Graviton5’s improved design increases speed and energy efficiency — beyond Moore’s law
articles.summary: A new chiplet architecture, custom die-to-die connectivity, and support for DDR5-8800 memory and the latest PCIe gen6 interconnects improve performance by 25% for general-purpose and agentic AI workloads.

articles.title: Real-world grounding in agentic AI
articles.summary: Four approaches can dramatically improve the performance and trustworthiness of AI agents in operational environments.

articles.title: Bridging intent and execution in agentic systems
articles.summary: The harnesses that mediate between models and tools in agentic systems are becoming their own performance bottleneck, but a few simple design principles can fix what ails them.

articles.title: Ground truth is a process, not a dataset
articles.summary: Automatically fact-checking long, AI-generated research reports poses new challenges — including benchmarking.

articles.title: How flat is replacing fat in AWS data center networks
articles.summary: “Quasi-random” network topologies and new passive optical components called ShuffleBoxes make more-efficient flat networks as practical as traditional “fat-tree” networks.

articles.title: Amazon Research Awards recipients announced
articles.summary: Awardees represent more than 49 universities in 11 countries. Recipients have access to Amazon public datasets, along with AWS AI/ML services and tools.

articles.title: Diverse reasoning traces teach LLMs to make better decisions
articles.summary: How to train language models to generate diverse, accurate reasoning paths using tokens that control distinct reasoning strategies.

articles.title: Making LLMs faster without sacrificing accuracy
articles.summary: A new scaling law that relates particular architectural choices to loss helps identify models that improve throughput by up to 47% with no loss of accuracy.

articles.title: Promptimus: Improving already good LLM prompts with zero manual engineering
articles.summary: By focusing on specific failure points and suggesting targeted solutions, a new automated prompt-engineering framework improves prompt performance without compromising existing functionality.

articles.title: Navigating uncertainty in Amazon's middle-mile network
articles.summary: Amazon engineers and scientists have created new tools to optimize delivery networks under uncertainty — and keep them adapting without missing a beat.

articles.title: How mechanism design theory helps optimize Amazon-vendor collaboration
articles.summary: Agentic mechanism enables Amazon and vendors to optimize supply chain management without disclosing private information.

articles.title: Building trust into AI
articles.summary: Amazon scientists and policy experts discuss how the company’s responsible-AI pipeline embeds safety and values throughout the AI development lifecycle.

articles.title: Preserving the privacy of AI training data
articles.summary: How we reproduced three attacks that extract private training data from AI models and the cryptographic defenses that stop them.

articles.title: How catastrophic is your LLM?
articles.summary: A new framework provides a statistical method for estimating the likelihood of catastrophic failures in large language models in adversarial conversations.

articles.title: Isabelle/HOL: The proof assistant behind the Nitro Isolation Engine
articles.summary: Isabelle/HOL's balance of expressiveness, automation, and scalability enabled the world's first formally verified cloud hypervisor.

articles.title: Customized Amazon Nova models improve molecular-property prediction in drug discovery
articles.summary: A single, optimized LLM unifies what previously required multiple models and can serve as a reasoning partner for medical chemists.

articles.title: AWS and Hopkins Engineering announce groundbreaking database for AI/ML antibody design
articles.summary: Built in collaboration with the Gray Lab at Johns Hopkins Whiting School of Engineering, the Antibody Developability Benchmark is powered by one of the most diverse antibody datasets in public literature, enabling transparent performance evaluation for AI-guided antibody design.

articles.title: How Amazon uses agentic AI for vulnerability detection at global scale
articles.summary: Amazon’s RuleForge system uses agentic AI to generate production-ready detection rules 336% faster than traditional methods.

articles.title: Verifying and optimizing post-quantum cryptography at Amazon
articles.summary: How automated reasoning reconciles the demands of security, performance, and maintainability.

articles.title: Improving quality and robustness in LLM-based text-to-speech systems
articles.summary: Low-rank adaptation, data augmentation, and chain-of-thought reasoning are among the techniques enabling accent-free polyglot outputs, improved expressiveness, and reliable synthesis.

articles.title: Formally verified AES-XTS: The first AES algorithm to join s2n-bignum
articles.summary: Simplifying and clarifying the assembly code for core operations enabled automated optimization and verification.

articles.title: Optimizing LoRA target module selection for efficient fine tuning
articles.summary: Ablation study clarifies trade-offs between accuracy and efficiency when using low-rank adaptation (LoRA) to fine-tune AI models.

articles.title: How agentic AI helps heal the systems we can’t replace
articles.summary: By learning the idiosyncrasies of accumulated layers of legacy systems, AI agents can preserve institutional knowledge and provide a unified interface to a range of services.

articles.title: Designing AI agents that know when to step back
articles.summary: As AI agents become more autonomous, the key challenge isn't what they can do; it's how to design the human side of the equation.
